---
title: "2026 GeoAI Arctic Challenge"
toc: true
toc-title: "Overview"
toc-location: "left"

---

## News 

- **July 1, 2026**: Competition kickoff. We are excited to announce the official launch of the 2026 GeoAI Arctic Challenge. Read welcoming words [here](./welcome-words.qmd).
- **July 1, 2026**: Public training data, hidden-label test images, starter scripts, and validation tools are released [here](https://huggingface.co/datasets/cyber2a/2026GeoAIArcticChallengeDataset).
- **August 15, 2026**: Benchmark phase starts.


The 2026 GeoAI Arctic Challenge invites participants to develop GeoAI and deep learning models for mapping retrogressive thaw slumps (RTS) from multimodal Arctic remote sensing data.

**Official Competition:** [2026 GeoAI Arctic Challenge on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/spaces/cyber2a/2026GeoAIArcticChallenge)

RTS are landslide-like landforms that occur when ice-rich permafrost thaws and collapses. As Arctic warming accelerates, these features are becoming more frequent and can reshape landscapes, alter hydrology, affect ecosystems, and mobilize long-frozen carbon.

![Retrogressive thaw slump example. *Credit: [Woodwell Climate Research Center](https://permafrost.woodwellclimate.org/using-machine-learning-and-high-resolution-satellite-imagery-to-detect-a-major-greenhouse-gas-emitting-climate-hazard/)*](./assets/rts.png){width=70% fig-align="center"}


## Challenge Task

Participants will train models to detect and delineate individual RTS features in Arctic image chips.

| Input | Output |
| --- | --- |
| Eight-band `.npz` image chip | One mask for each predicted RTS instance |

The challenge is an **instance segmentation** task. Training labels are provided in [COCO instance segmentation format](https://cocodataset.org/#format-data). Test labels are hidden and used by the official scorer.

## Why This Matters

Mapping RTS manually across Arctic regions is difficult because these landforms can be small, visually subtle, and dynamically changing. Automated mapping can help scientists monitor permafrost degradation, improve environmental change detection, and better understand climate-driven Arctic landscape transformation.

## How To Start

1. Form a team of 1 to 5 members.
2. Create a Hugging Face account for each team member.
3. Complete the team registration form [here](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1KUGGduTATQK3wUoRRzlClt3PU11XaHCNnyTB1SyBVfM/).
4. Include the exact Hugging Face username for the Team Leader and each team member in the registration form.
5. Visit the [official competition page](https://huggingface.co/spaces/cyber2a/2026GeoAIArcticChallenge).
6. Click **Login with Hugging Face** in the left sidebar to sign in and link your competition account.
7. Wait for organizer approval. Approved usernames will be added to the competition.
8. After approval, use **New submission** to upload predictions and **My submissions** to review team submission history.

**Note:** Unregistered users can browse the competition pages and leaderboard, but only approved Hugging Face usernames can submit.

## Important Dates

| Event | Date |
| --- | --- |
| Competition opens | July 1, 2026 |
| Dataset released | July 1, 2026 |
| Benchmark phase starts | August 15, 2026 |
| Final submission deadline | January 31, 2027 |
| Winners announced | March 1, 2027 |

## Awards

Top-performing teams will receive cash awards and recognition:

| Place | Award |
| --- | --- |
| 1st place | $1,000 |
| 2nd place | $500 |
| 3rd place | $200 |

Selected participants may also be invited to contribute to an outcome publication and future research collaborations.

## Dataset Preview

The dataset contains high-resolution, multimodal satellite image chips paired with RTS instance segmentation labels. RGB composites are shown below for visualization only; the modeling input contains eight channels.

| Input: RGB composite | Target: RTS instances |
| :---: | :---: |
| ![](assets/rts_1_rgb.png) | ![](assets/rts_1_polygons.png) |
| ![](assets/rts_2_rgb.png) | ![](assets/rts_2_polygons.png) |

## Partners

::: {layout="[[30, 30, 25, 20]]"}

![](../assets/clients/ASU.png)

![](../assets/clients/Woodwell.png)

![](../assets/clients/ArcticDataCenter.png)

![](../assets/clients/Ncsa_logo.gif)

:::

## Sponsors

![](../assets/clients/NSF.png){width=20%}

## Contact

For competition support, contact [wenwen@asu.edu](mailto:wenwen@asu.edu) or [chiayuhsu@asu.edu](mailto:chiayuhsu@asu.edu).

For technical questions that may help other teams, use the [discussion](https://huggingface.co/spaces/cyber2a/2026GeoAIArcticChallenge/discussions) area on the competition page. You may also contact the organizing team by email.
